fetchedPapers.title: LLM Detection as an Intervention: Downstream Impact under Strategic User Behavior
fetchedPapers.abstract: As LLM adoption becomes more widespread, there is a growing interest in detecting LLM-generated content, for example through LLM detection tools and through heuristics based on language patterns. Detectors operate as an intervention that steers not only the detected attribute itself, but also downstream metrics such as LLM usage and output quality. In this work, we demonstrate how imperfect LLM detectors lead to counterintuitive impacts on these downstream metrics, by distorting how users are incentivized to use LLMs in their workflow. We develop a stylized model which captures how users strategically choose how much to use the LLM and how to post-process content to reduce the detected attribute. Using this model, we show that LLM detection can counterintuitively lead humans to increase their LLM usage. Moreover, even when reducing the detected attribute improves output quality, we find that introducing an LLM detector can lead users to produce lower quality outputs. In contrast, we show that detectors result in a clean "rise-then-fall" pattern for the detected attribute, which we empirically reproduce for word frequencies on arXiv abstracts. Altogether, our work illustrates how LLM detection can distort LLM usage and output quality, uncovering failure modes when LLM detectors operate as an intervention on these downstream metrics.

fetchedPapers.title: Stability in Combinatorial Markets with Side Payments
fetchedPapers.abstract: Combinatorial markets provide a general framework for trading bundles of indivisible goods. Building on the combinatorial market models of Bikhchandani & Ostroy (2002) and Bichler & Waldherr (2017), we introduce explicit side payments, thereby allowing restricted transfers of utility among subsets of agents and capturing different forms of financial collusion. This extension results in a large number of seemingly distinct market settings. However, we establish a systematic classification of their expressive power. Most notably, we show that higher-order market settings (i.e., those with more personalized prices and less clearinghouse power) are essentially equivalent in terms of market properties when side payments are permitted between buyers. An analogous collapse occurs for the third- and higher-order settings when side payments are permitted between sellers. In contrast, we identify a fundamental structural separation between the second- and third-order settings. To analyze stability in these environments, we generalize the classical concepts of stability with and without transferable utility (TU and NTU) to a partition-based notion of restricted transferability (the $T$-core). We relate stable outcomes across different settings to appropriate stability notions, identify market instances in which partially transferable utility yields a better (or any) stable outcome, and show that under personalized pricing, all stability notions collapse to NTU-stability.

fetchedPapers.title: Hospitals/Residents with Inseparable Couples: Finding a Coalition-Stable Assignment Is NP-Hard
fetchedPapers.abstract: In recent work on course allocation, Rodríguez and Manlove consider the complexity of finding a stable assignment under four notions of stability, including two coalitional notions. In one case, which they call pair-size stability, they show that a stable assignment always exists and they provide a polynomial-time algorithm to find one. In a second case, called pair stability, they observe that an earlier NP-hardness result of McDermid and Manlove holds for a special case of course allocation called Hospitals/Residents with Sizes ($\mbox{HRS}$). In a third case, called first-coalition stability, they use a reduction from $\mbox{HRS}$ to show it is NP-hard to find a stable assignment. They leave open the complexity of finding a stable assignment under so-called coalition stability. Building on ideas from McDermid and Manlove, we resolve the open problem of Rodríguez and Manlove by showing that it is NP-hard to find a coalition-stable assignment for $\mbox{HRS}$. Indeed, our proof shows that the problem remains NP-hard when the hospital capacities and resident sizes are at most two. Accordingly, our NP-hardness result applies to the special case of $\mbox{HRS}$ known as Hospitals/Residents with Inseparable Couples ($\mbox{HRIC}$). Finally, we introduce a novel and natural notion of coalitional stability for both $\mbox{HRS}$ and course allocation, and we show that our NP-hardness result extends to this notion, which we call unitwise-coalition stability.

fetchedPapers.title: Market tallies: minimal information for efficient trade
fetchedPapers.abstract: This paper studies how much public information is needed to implement efficient trade in dynamic markets with privately informed sellers and buyers. An institution compares a certified statistic of market composition with the statistic implied by agents' reports. Truthful reporting is supported when the statistic changes after every unilateral change in reported type. When all market compositions are possible, the least number of public announcements is {K,L}, where K is the number of seller qualities and L the number of buyer types. The certificate must rely on information outside the reports it checks. The paper also shows that information sufficient to discipline reports need not coordinate buyers across limited capacity. Posted-price implementation may require certified capacities and a clearing rule.

fetchedPapers.title: A Coordination Theory of NHS Whistleblowing Failure
fetchedPapers.abstract: Persistent whistleblowing failure in hierarchical healthcare organisations is typically attributed to insufficient legal protection for reporters or inadequate managerial incentives to investigate. This diagnosis is argued to be structurally incomplete. A repeated three-player game among a whistleblower, Trust management, and a colleague group is solved via global games techniques to obtain a unique equilibrium, identifying three mechanisms existing policy neither recognises nor addresses.

fetchedPapers.title: Curriculum design in the age of AI
fetchedPapers.abstract: I develop a model of learning-by-doing and curriculum design, and use it to study the impact of artificial intelligence (AI). A myopic student faces a sequence of tasks that he can work on or delegate to AI. Work requires costly effort but builds skill; delegation requires no effort but builds no skill. A teacher designs a task sequence ("curriculum") that maximizes the student's skill development given his choices to work or delegate. Without AI, the teacher makes earlier tasks more effort-intensive and later tasks more skill-intensive. With AI, the teacher must distort the curriculum to incentivize effort, leading to less skill development. If AI complements effort, then improvements in AI quality make high-skill students learn faster but low-skill students learn slower.

paper.title: Stability in Combinatorial Markets with Side Payments

rawOutput.titleZh: 带侧向支付的组合市场中的稳定性

summarySections.backgroundAndQuestion: 组合市场中买卖双方交易物品束时，传统模型难以刻画现实中存在的非正式资金转移行为，如企业联盟间的补贴或买家之间的协调付款。现有研究在价格个性化程度与清算机构权力之间存在结构性割裂，无法统一解释不同市场层级下的稳定性差异。本文旨在填补这一理论空白，探究侧向支付如何影响市场层级间的表达能力与稳定结果。
summarySections.modelAndSetup: 模型基于Bikhchandani & Ostroy的四级组合市场框架，并引入第五级设置，区分不同层级下清算机构对包裹重组与标签调整的能力。允许买方、卖方或双方内部进行受限的侧向支付，形成不同的效用转移结构。通过划分代理人间的支付权限，构建了一个从完全不可转让到部分可转让效用的连续谱系，用于分析不同限制下的市场表现。
summarySections.contributionsAndResults: 论文发现，在允许充分侧向支付时，高阶市场设置（如第四、五级）在收益实现上是等价的，打破了原有层级间的严格区分。特别指出第二级与第三级之间存在本质分离。同时提出T-核（T-core）作为统一的稳定性概念，揭示在个性化定价下所有稳定性定义会退化为非转移效用（NTU）稳定性，表明价格灵活性可替代外部协作。
summarySections.methodsAndTechniques: 作者采用整数规划对偶框架刻画市场均衡，并利用分层等价关系比较不同设置的表达力。关键技术包括通过流网络重构侧向支付路径，将外部转移内化为市场价格调整。借助联合划分（join of partitions）统一处理稳定性与支付限制的关系，从而将复杂的T-RTU稳定性转化为标准NTU稳定性分析。
summarySections.limitationsAndReadingValue: 模型依赖强理性与完全信息假设，且等价性结论对低层级市场的适用性有限。结果高度依赖于特定的定价结构与支付约束组合，实际机制实现可能面临信息与执行成本挑战。最值得阅读的是其对市场层级的系统分类、T-core的推广定义及关于个性化定价消解协作需求的核心洞见。

paper.title: Market tallies: minimal information for efficient trade

rawOutput.titleZh: 市场统计：高效交易所需的最少信息

summarySections.backgroundAndQuestion: 传统动态市场通过价格和等待时间逐步揭示私有信息，但会延迟有效交易。本文问：一个外部机构需要公开多少信息，才能一次性实现无延迟的高效交易？核心问题是，在买卖双方均拥有私有类型的情况下，如何用最少的公共信息披露来激励真实报告，同时避免因信息不足导致的协调失败。
summarySections.modelAndSetup: 模型考虑有限数量的卖家（各有私有质量）和买家（各有私有估值类型）。市场机制无法直接观测类型，但可依赖一个独立来源发布的市场构成统计量（如各类型的数量）。机制比较该统计量与代理人报告推导出的统计量，仅当一致时才允许交易。关键设定是统计量不必披露完整构成，只需能检测单边虚假报告。
summarySections.contributionsAndResults: 论文发现，只要统计量在任意单个代理改变报告时都会变化，就能支持真实报告作为均衡。当所有市场构成都可能时，所需最小公告数为卖方质量数K与买方类型数L的最大值，远小于完整构成的组合数。这表明极简的公共信息（如模余数）足以支撑高效交易。
summarySections.methodsAndTechniques: 证明依赖于对‘分离性’统计量的构造与计数论证：通过模运算构造仅需max{K,L}个值的统计量，并证明任何更小的集合都无法区分所有单代理变更的构成。关键技巧是将问题转化为图上邻接点染色，利用独立源确保统计量不可被报告操纵。
summarySections.limitationsAndReadingValue: 结果依赖有限市场假设，在连续统经济中不成立；且仅实现弱实施，可能存在无效均衡。机制仍需额外协调（如清仓规则）来避免拥堵。最值得读的是其对信息披露与验证的分离洞察，以及用极小信息解决逆向选择的新范式。

paper.title: Hospitals/Residents with Inseparable Couples: Finding a Coalition-Stable Assignment Is NP-Hard

rawOutput.titleZh: Hospitals/Residents with Inseparable Couples: Finding a Coalition-Stable Assignment Is NP-Hard

summarySections.backgroundAndQuestion: 为什么要研究这个问题？在医院-居民匹配问题中，当存在不可拆分的伴侣（如夫妻）时，如何找到一个稳定的分配方案是一个长期难题。已有研究提出了多种稳定性定义，但关于‘联盟稳定性’是否存在及计算复杂性的开放问题仍未解决。本文旨在填补这一理论空白，探究该问题的可解性边界。
summarySections.modelAndSetup: 作者如何把问题抽象成模型？研究基于Hospitals/Residents with Sizes（HRS）框架，每个居民有大小，医院有容量限制。联盟稳定性要求不存在一组居民和一家医院，使得这组居民更偏好该医院，且医院可通过替换现有居民来容纳他们。模型通过引入大小、偏好列表和容量约束，刻画了资源受限下的集体偏离可能性。
summarySections.contributionsAndResults: 论文真正证明或发现了什么？作者证明寻找联盟稳定的分配是NP难的，即使在医院容量和居民大小至多为2的特殊情况下也成立。此外，展示了一个仅含4名居民和3家医院的小实例不存在联盟稳定分配，从而否定了一般存在性。结果表明该问题不仅计算困难，且解可能根本不存在。
summarySections.methodsAndTechniques: 作者是怎么得到这些结果的？采用从(2,2)-E3-SAT到HRS-C的多项式时间归约，构造了模块化的‘不稳定’‘文字’和‘双稳’小工具。通过偏好图表示各组件，并利用引理分析子结构间的阻塞联盟传播关系，最终拼接出完整实例。技术核心在于精确控制局部稳定性和全局不稳定性之间的张力。
summarySections.limitationsAndReadingValue: 这篇论文该如何评价、是否值得继续读？虽然结果依赖于特定稳定性定义，且机制不易直接部署，但其归约构造清晰、模块化强，为后续研究提供了可复用的技术框架。最值得读的是其小工具设计思想与对联盟稳定性的精细刻画，对于理解匹配市场的复杂性与伴侣影响具有深刻启示。

finalPaper.titleZh: Hospitals/Residents with Inseparable Couples: Finding a Coalition-Stable Assignment Is NP-Hard

summarySections.backgroundAndQuestion: 为什么要研究这个问题？在医院-居民匹配问题中，当存在不可拆分的伴侣（如夫妻）时，如何找到一个稳定的分配方案是一个长期难题。已有研究提出了多种稳定性定义，但关于‘联盟稳定性’是否存在及计算复杂性的开放问题仍未解决。本文旨在填补这一理论空白，探究该问题的可解性边界。
summarySections.modelAndSetup: 作者如何把问题抽象成模型？研究基于Hospitals/Residents with Sizes（HRS）框架，每个居民有大小，医院有容量限制。联盟稳定性要求不存在一组居民和一家医院，使得这组居民更偏好该医院，且医院可通过替换现有居民来容纳他们。模型通过引入大小、偏好列表和容量约束，刻画了资源受限下的集体偏离可能性。
summarySections.contributionsAndResults: 论文真正证明或发现了什么？作者证明寻找联盟稳定的分配是NP难的，即使在医院容量和居民大小至多为2的特殊情况下也成立。此外，展示了一个仅含4名居民和3家医院的小实例不存在联盟稳定分配，从而否定了一般存在性。结果表明该问题不仅计算困难，且解可能根本不存在。
summarySections.methodsAndTechniques: 作者是怎么得到这些结果的？采用从(2,2)-E3-SAT到HRS-C的多项式时间归约，构造了模块化的‘不稳定’‘文字’和‘双稳’小工具。通过偏好图表示各组件，并利用引理分析子结构间的阻塞联盟传播关系，最终拼接出完整实例。技术核心在于精确控制局部稳定性和全局不稳定性之间的张力。
summarySections.limitationsAndReadingValue: 这篇论文该如何评价、是否值得继续读？虽然结果依赖于特定稳定性定义，且机制不易直接部署，但其归约构造清晰、模块化强，为后续研究提供了可复用的技术框架。最值得读的是其小工具设计思想与对联盟稳定性的精细刻画，对于理解匹配市场的复杂性与伴侣影响具有深刻启示。

paper.title: LLM Detection as an Intervention: Downstream Impact under Strategic User Behavior

rawOutput.titleZh: LLM 检测作为干预手段：战略用户行为下的下游影响

summarySections.backgroundAndQuestion: 随着大语言模型（LLM）广泛应用，机构试图通过检测工具识别AI生成内容，以减少LLM使用或提升输出质量。然而，这类检测会改变用户的激励结构，导致他们战略性调整工作流程，例如修改文本以规避检测。本文关注的核心问题是：这种干预在考虑用户策略性反应后，是否真的能达到预期效果？现有研究常忽略用户行为的动态响应，从而可能误判检测的实际影响。
summarySections.modelAndSetup: 作者构建了一个简化模型来刻画用户在检测压力下的决策过程。每个用户决定将多少任务交给LLM完成，并选择是否对输出进行后处理以降低被检测到的风险。检测器基于某一维度特征（如特定词汇频率）进行阈值判定，而内容质量则由多个维度共同决定。用户权衡产出质量、被罚风险、创作成本和修改成本，最终选择最优策略组合，形成一个带激励响应的干预分析框架。
summarySections.contributionsAndResults: 论文发现，尽管检测器能有效降低其直接针对的特征（如‘LLM感’），但它可能导致反直觉的下游后果：一些用户反而增加LLM使用比例，甚至产出更低质量的内容。这揭示了检测机制的失败模式——当用户可通过后处理规避惩罚时，干预可能扭曲真实目标。特别地，当LLM在非检测维度表现更优时，用户倾向于利用这一点，即使需付出修改代价。
summarySections.methodsAndTechniques: 作者采用战略分类的经典分析路径，结合效用最大化框架推导用户最优响应。关键技术在于刻画检测边界附近的局部行为，利用凸性与连续性论证工作分配与后处理之间的权衡关系。通过构造反例与充分必要条件，证明了LLM使用上升与质量下降现象的存在性，并借助极值分析展示‘先升后降’模式的普遍性，整个论证依托于对用户优化问题的精细刻画。
summarySections.limitationsAndReadingValue: 模型假设用户完全知晓检测规则且修改成本固定，这在现实中可能不成立。此外，质量函数的可分性与低维嵌入限制了复杂交互的表达。尽管如此，该研究的价值在于揭示了检测作为干预的本质矛盾：它引发的行为扭曲可能抵消甚至逆转政策意图。最值得阅读的是其核心洞见——将检测视为激励机制而非单纯技术工具，为后续机制设计提供了理论起点。

finalPaper.titleZh: LLM 检测作为干预手段：战略用户行为下的下游影响

paper.title: Curriculum design in the age of AI

rawOutput.titleZh: 人工智能时代的课程设计

summarySections.backgroundAndQuestion: 随着人工智能在教育中的广泛应用，学生倾向于将任务委托给AI以节省精力，但这可能削弱其技能发展。论文关注的核心问题是：教师应如何设计课程序列（任务难度安排），才能在学生可能偷懒的情况下，最大化其长期技能积累？这触及教育激励的根本矛盾——即时效率与长期能力培养之间的权衡。
summarySections.modelAndSetup: 模型包含一个短视的学生和一位前瞻性的教师。学生面对一系列任务，可选择亲自完成（耗费努力但提升技能）或委托AI（零成本但无成长）。教师设计每个任务的努力密集度，以激励学生投入努力。技能通过努力累积，且努力与技能互补，形成动态增强效应。教师的目标是最大化最终技能水平。
summarySections.contributionsAndResults: 论文发现，在无AI干扰时，最优课程应从努力密集转向技能密集。但当AI存在时，教师必须扭曲课程，使早期任务更侧重技能以防止学生放弃努力，导致整体技能增长下降。此外，AI质量提升会加速高技能学生的学习，却阻碍低技能学生，加剧不平等。
summarySections.methodsAndTechniques: 作者通过求解动态优化问题刻画最优课程路径。关键在于分析学生参与约束下的教师决策：比较第一优（无委托）与第二优（需激励）情形。利用最佳反应函数和隐函数定理，推导出努力强度、技能阈值与参数间的单调关系，并通过分段极值论证非单调课程结构的存在性。
summarySections.limitationsAndReadingValue: 模型假设学生完全短视且AI仅作为替代工具，忽略了合作使用AI的可能性及长期学习动机。尽管如此，论文的价值在于清晰揭示了AI对教育激励结构的破坏性影响及其内生应对机制。最值得阅读的是其简洁有力的动态激励框架和关于‘高质量AI反而拉大差距’的反直觉洞见。

fetchedPapers.title: Spiking Neural Networks for fMRI-Based Visual Semantic Decoding
fetchedPapers.abstract: Functional magnetic resonance imaging (fMRI)-based visual decoding aims to recover visual information from measured brain activity, commonly by mapping fMRI responses into latent visual features for downstream decoding tasks. Most existing methods learn mappings from fMRI responses to visual features extracted by artificial neural networks (ANNs), yet it remains unclear whether ANN-derived features provide suitable targets for brain decoding. In this study, we investigate spiking neural network (SNN)-derived visual features as alternative targets for fMRI-based visual decoding. We compare an ANN baseline with four SNN variants from the same architectural family, which differ in their spiking dynamics. To isolate the effect of the target features, all models use the same L2-regularized linear fMRI-to-feature decoder, while only the feature vectors used as regression targets are varied. Compared with the ANN baseline, SNN-derived features exhibit stronger alignment with fMRI responses and improve visual semantic decoding performance. For instance, on the GoD dataset, SNN-derived features reduce feature-prediction error from 0.7707 to 0.0282 and improve top-1 semantic decoding accuracy from 0.1800 to 0.4400. Ablation results further indicate that both spiking neural dynamics and temporal simulation steps contribute to the observed advantage. These findings support SNN-derived features as effective brain-decodable visual representations and highlight target feature design as an important component of fMRI-based visual decoding.

fetchedPapers.title: Towards chemistries in dynamical systems
fetchedPapers.abstract: Chemistry describes aspects of the universe in terms of molecules and their reactions. In this exploratory work we present a way to describe aspects of any dynamical system in similar terms. To describe a dynamical system in this way three decisions have to be made. The first is how many different "places" there are at which molecules or chemical species can occur; the second is how to determine the species present (or not) at each place; and the third is the set of transitions and reactions that can occur between the species in the various places. For these choices to be compatible with the state update of the dynamical system each state must be able to determine transitions that take the currently occurring molecules to those occurring in the updated state. We also propose an additional requirement that there is always a unique way to choose the least amount of transitions occurring during state updates. We discuss gliders in the game of life cellular and argue that when following their definition of according to Randall Beer they satisfy the additional criterion as well. We also point out some issues with the approach.

fetchedPapers.title: How the fly holds a single goal: normalization, not selection, in Drosophila FC2
fetchedPapers.abstract: A walking fly steers toward a goal direction, held as a bump of activity across the FC2 neurons of the fan-shaped body. These neurons also inhibit one another over distance, more strongly the farther apart they are, a feedback proposed to keep the fly on a single goal. We asked, from the connectome, what circuit produces this inhibition, and whether it lets FC2 actively choose one goal among competitors (a winner-take-all) or simply keeps a goal set elsewhere as one clean bump. Tracing the wiring in a single FlyWire brain, we find the inhibition is almost entirely global: four FB5A cells inhibit every FC2 neuron roughly equally, with a smaller, distance-dependent contribution from hDelta interneurons and a negligible direct component. A ring-attractor winner-take-all (the kind the compass uses) requires local recurrent excitation that the FC2 wiring lacks, so this geometry cannot build one; and across a range of dynamical models, including a spiking network, no version of the circuit locks onto a winner at the connectome-scaled reference coupling. FC2 therefore normalizes an externally set goal rather than selecting it, with FB5A likely acting as the global normalizer, much as the APL neuron does in the mushroom body. We are explicit about two open points: a different mechanism, mutual inhibition between two competing goals (which hDelta supplies), could in principle select at very strong coupling, and we bound rather than exclude it; and FB5A's inhibitory identity is a low-confidence prediction of the connectome's transmitter classifier, not yet measured, and likely not GABAergic. We then ask where the goal is actually set: the connectome nominates an upstream hDelta network and rules out the leading proposed alternative, whose neurons supply under 0.2% of FC2's input. Finally, we propose a direct experiment, silencing FB5A while imaging FC2, that would test the account.

In this study, we investigate spiking neural network (SNN)-derived visual features as alternative targets for fMRI-based visual decoding. Ablation results further indicate that both spiking neural dynamics and temporal simulation steps contribute to the observed advantage. These findings support SNN-derived features as effective brain-decodable visual representations and highlight target feature design as an important component of fMRI-based visual decoding.

paper.title: How the fly holds a single goal: normalization, not selection, in Drosophila FC2

rawOutput.titleZh: 果蝇如何保持单一目标：Drosophila FC2中的归一化而非选择

summarySections.backgroundAndQuestion: 果蝇在导航时需维持一个目标方向，表现为FC2神经元上的活动‘峰’。过去认为这种单峰可能是通过类似‘胜者通吃’的竞争机制实现的，即多个候选目标相互抑制最终选出唯一胜者。但该机制是否真实存在尚无连接组证据支持。本文质疑这一假设，探究FC2环路究竟是主动选择目标，还是仅对外部设定的目标进行归一化表达。
summarySections.modelAndSetup: 研究基于果蝇全脑连接组（FlyWire和hemibrain）解析FC2神经元间的反馈抑制通路，分析其空间模式。通过构建多种动态模型（包括率模型、尖峰网络、除法归一化模型），模拟双目标竞争情境下的活动演化，并设计种子盆地测试来检验是否存在历史依赖性的锁定行为。同时追踪上游输入来源，评估不同回路对目标设定的潜在贡献。
summarySections.contributionsAndResults: 论文发现FC2的抑制主要来自全局均匀的FB5A细胞和远端增强的h△通路，缺乏局部递归兴奋，无法支持环状吸引子式的胜者通吃机制。模型验证显示系统不具备双稳态，不能自主锁定单一目标。相反，FC2更可能执行归一化功能，保持外部输入目标的清晰表达。上游h△网络被提名为目标持有者，而Lanz等人提出的hAK-PFG环路因输入占比极低被排除。
summarySections.methodsAndTechniques: 作者采用连接组解构方法，量化三条可能的反馈路径（直接、FB5A介导、h△介导）的空间依赖性。利用双目标驱动和种子初始化测试检测系统是否具备选择能力。构建包含真实连接权重的除法归一化模型，模拟活动形状与幅度变化。通过跨两个独立连接组复制关键结构特征增强结论稳健性，并提出可证伪的沉默FB5A实验以验证归一化假说。
summarySections.limitationsAndReadingValue: 核心结论依赖于连接组预测的FB5A抑制性身份，但其递质分类置信度低且同类细胞多为谷氨酸能，实际功能仍待实验证实。h△通路在高增益下可能存在部分选择能力，尚未完全排除。尽管如此，本文对‘归一化vs选择’的区分框架、连接组驱动的机制推断方法及明确的实验预言极具阅读价值，尤其适合关注神经计算原理与行为控制机制的研究者。

finalPaper.titleZh: 果蝇如何保持单一目标：Drosophila FC2中的归一化而非选择

paper.title: Spiking Neural Networks for fMRI-Based Visual Semantic Decoding

rawOutput.titleZh: 基于fMRI的视觉语义解码中的脉冲神经网络

summarySections.backgroundAndQuestion: 现有基于fMRI的视觉解码通常将大脑活动映射到人工神经网络（ANN）提取的视觉特征，但这类特征虽在计算机视觉中有效，未必适合模拟人脑的信息表达方式。本文关注的问题是：更贴近生物神经机制的脉冲神经网络（SNN）所提取的动态、稀疏特征，是否比静态密集的ANN特征更易于从fMRI信号中恢复，并提升语义解码性能。
summarySections.modelAndSetup: 研究采用统一的L2正则化线性解码器，将fMRI体素响应映射到不同模型生成的视觉特征空间。输入为受试者观看图像时采集的fMRI数据，输出为目标特征向量。比较对象包括一个ResNet-18 ANN和四种同构的SNN变体（LIF、PSN、MPSN、BuSNN），所有特征均用于下游语义分类、图像检索和重建任务，在GoD、NSD等多个公开fMRI数据集上评估。
summarySections.contributionsAndResults: 论文发现SNN衍生的特征显著优于ANN基线：不仅与fMRI响应的体素级对齐更强，而且在语义层面预测误差更低，Top-1分类准确率从0.18提升至0.44。这表明SNN特征作为中间表示更具‘可解码性’，其优势源于脉冲动态本身而非模型容量差异，挑战了传统依赖ANN特征的默认做法。
summarySections.methodsAndTechniques: 核心方法在于控制变量设计——固定解码器结构与训练流程，仅改变目标视觉特征来源。SNN通过多时间步的脉冲累积生成特征，其时间动态和神经状态更新机制（如膜电位积分、记忆传播、爆发式放电）增强了与fMRI信号的时间整合特性的匹配。消融实验证明时间步数和脉冲机制均影响最终性能。
summarySections.limitationsAndReadingValue: 研究局限于特定ResNet架构下的SNN与ANN对比，未涵盖Transformer等现代架构；且重建基于语义引导生成而非像素级反演。尽管如此，其严谨的对照实验揭示了特征设计对脑解码的关键影响，值得细读其问题设定、消融分析与对齐评估方法，尤其对脑-机接口与神经表示学习方向有启发意义。
